skill.name: frontend-design
skill.description: Create distinctive, production-grade frontend interfaces with strong visual direction, refined interaction design, and working implementation details.
skill.summary: Build polished web interfaces with bold, context-aware visual design.
skill.author: anthropics

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported weak cryptography and reconnaissance patterns, but manual review found only natural-language design guidance in SKILL.md. No executable code, command execution, network access, filesystem access, prompt injection, or data exfiltration behavior was found.

low_findings.title: Static Pattern False Positives in Prose
low_findings.description: The reported weak cryptographic algorithm and system reconnaissance matches occur in ordinary frontend design instructions, such as the skill name, design guidance, and references to system fonts. These lines do not contain executable code, algorithms, shell commands, network calls, or credential access.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review confirmed the flagged lines are prose-only guidance. No code paths or operational instructions implement cryptography or system reconnaissance.

content.value_statement: Generic interfaces often feel forgettable and disconnected from their product context. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code create polished frontend work with a clear visual direction.
actual_capabilities: Guides creation of web components, pages, applications, and user interfaces.
Encourages a clear aesthetic direction before implementation begins.
Provides criteria for typography, color, motion, layout, and background detail.
Supports HTML, CSS, JavaScript, React, Vue, and similar frontend stacks.
Promotes production-grade working code rather than static visual mockups.
Helps avoid common generic AI design patterns and repetitive palettes.
limitations: Does not include runnable scripts, templates, assets, or component libraries.
Does not test generated interfaces for accessibility or browser compatibility by itself.
Depends on the model and project environment for final implementation quality.
Provides design guidance, not brand strategy or formal user research.

use_cases.title: Create a Product Dashboard
use_cases.description: Build a polished operational dashboard with clear hierarchy, dense information, and refined interaction states.

use_cases.title: Prototype a Marketing Page
use_cases.description: Generate a memorable first screen and supporting sections that match a product category and audience.

use_cases.title: Improve an Existing Interface
use_cases.description: Refine typography, spacing, motion, and visual details while preserving the existing frontend framework.

prompt_templates.title: Beginner Interface Build
prompt_templates.scenario: A user needs a new page or component with basic context.
prompt_templates.prompt: Build a frontend interface for [purpose]. The audience is [audience]. Use the existing project stack and make it feel polished and distinctive.

prompt_templates.title: Visual Direction Exploration
prompt_templates.scenario: A user wants a stronger concept before coding.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a frontend concept for [product]. Choose one clear aesthetic direction, explain the design choices briefly, then implement the interface.

prompt_templates.title: Existing UI Refinement
prompt_templates.scenario: A user has an interface that needs higher design quality.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this frontend and improve its typography, spacing, color, motion, and component states while keeping the current functionality intact.

prompt_templates.title: Advanced Product Experience
prompt_templates.scenario: A user needs a complete, context-specific application surface.
prompt_templates.prompt: Design and implement a production-grade [application type] for [users]. Include responsive layout, interaction states, motion details, and a cohesive visual system.

output_examples.input: Build a dashboard for a logistics team tracking delayed shipments.
output_examples.output: A dense operational dashboard with shipment queues, status filters, clear alert colors, compact tables, and restrained motion for state changes.

output_examples.input: Create a landing page for an experimental audio tool.
output_examples.output: A bold product page with expressive typography, waveform-inspired visual details, responsive sections, and interactive controls that match the audio theme.

output_examples.input: Improve the design of an existing React settings page.
output_examples.output: A refined settings interface with clearer grouping, better form states, improved spacing, consistent controls, and a calmer visual rhythm.

best_practices: Provide the product purpose, audience, stack, and any existing design constraints.
Ask for a specific aesthetic direction when brand fit matters.
Review generated UI in real viewport sizes before accepting the result.
anti_patterns: Do not request only a vague modern design without product context.
Do not accept decorative effects that reduce readability or task completion.
Do not use this skill as a substitute for accessibility testing.

faq.question: What does this skill help create?
faq.answer: It helps create frontend components, pages, applications, and interfaces with stronger visual direction and working implementation details.

faq.question: Does it generate code?
faq.answer: Yes. It guides the assistant to implement working frontend code in stacks such as HTML, CSS, JavaScript, React, and Vue.

faq.question: Is it only for new projects?
faq.answer: No. It can also improve existing interfaces by refining layout, typography, color, motion, and component states.

faq.question: Does it include assets or templates?
faq.answer: No. The skill is a guidance file and does not include bundled assets, scripts, or reusable component templates.

faq.question: Can it replace design review?
faq.answer: No. Human review is still important for accessibility, brand fit, usability, and implementation quality.

faq.question: Which tools are supported?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
